oath: I shall keep the king I serve from harm, obey his commands, keep his secrets, counsel him when he asks it and no other time, and give my life for his if need be — forsaking lands, titles, and a wife of my own, all my days.

deeds.description: Raised to the Kingsguard in his seventeenth year; in time rose to Lord Commander.
Wielded the ancestral Valyrian steel blade Dark Sister.
Pulled alive from a snakepit by his cousin, who would later be crowned — the vipers refused to strike him.
Fought Lord Cregan Stark to a narrow, hard-won victory.
Won the tourney at Riverrun in his twenty-first year, unhorsing Ser Terrence Toyne in the final tilt.
Born to obscure origins in a city's poorest quarter; squired to a wandering hedge knight who later knighted him in his sixteenth year.
Defended a Dornish lady's honor at a tourney and stood a trial by seven for it.
Took a young prince on as his own squire, though he chose to remain a hedge knight himself.
Uncovered a plot among certain lords to raise a pretender against the crown, and shielded a prince from the danger.
Rose to Lord Commander of the Kingsguard, raised to the post by the king he had once squired for.
Led the honor guard that escorted a royal uncle to take the black at the Wall.
Rescued a lord's daughter after her ship was seized by island raiders.
Defeated every challenger at a tourney held in his own honor, and crowned a commoner as Queen of Love and Beauty.
Died in the mysterious fire that claimed his king and a young prince alongside him.

endDate: died in the Red Mountains of Dorne

deeds.description: Defended the honor and property of a Dornish princess.
Broke three lances in a famed tourney and fought with distinction in its melee.
Commanded the Kingsguard for many years before yielding the post due to injury.
Sent by his king to find a fugitive prince in the wake of a rebellion.
Died in the Red Mountains of Dorne beside two sworn brothers, refusing to bend the knee to the new king.

endDate: died in the Red Mountains of Dorne

deeds.description: Bore the ancestral greatsword said to be forged from a fallen star.
Broke a dozen lances against a crown prince in a famed tourney, standing undefeated by all but him.
Broke the siege of a northern port by sea-raiders and saved a northern lord from drowning.
Helped destroy a notorious outlaw brotherhood, slaying its most feared knight in single combat.
Knighted a young squire upon the field of battle.
Died in the Red Mountains of Dorne beside his sworn brothers, at the close of a rebellion.
Fought as an unproven mystery knight at ten, and was unmasked after his defeat.
Knighted for valor after unhorsing a sitting Lord Commander of the Kingsguard while disguised as a mystery knight.
Slew the last of a line of pretenders in single combat during a war fought across the narrow sea.
Named to the Kingsguard in his twenty-third year.
Carried his king to safety through an arrow wound of his own.
Avenged the murder of a slain sworn brother.
Rescued a lady and her septa from outlaws, helping bring down their leaders.
Stood champion at a royal tourney, unhorsing half a dozen great lords and a crown prince in turn.
Raised to Lord Commander by the king who took the throne after the one he had served.
Dismissed from service in his old age by a boy-king.
Squired against an outlaw brotherhood; knighted in the field at fifteen for valor.
Chosen for the Kingsguard that same year.
Slew the king he had sworn to protect during the sack of the capital, earning the name "Kingslayer."
Pardoned for the killing by the king who took the throne after.
Captured in the field after a defeat, then released on an oath to protect two highborn sisters.
Lost his sword hand to his captors during that search.
Took back his family's ancestral river seat without further bloodshed.
Fought beyond the old kings' road against the army of the dead, holding a castle through impossible odds.
Died shielding his queen amid the ashes of a fallen city.

oath: I shall keep the king I serve from harm, obey his commands, keep his secrets, counsel him when he asks it and no other time, and give my life for his if need be — forsaking lands, titles, and a wife of my own, all my days.

deeds.description: Rose to Lord Commander of the Kingsguard, and held it through decades of relative peace.

Trained Prince Jacaelon Targaryen rigorously during Ser Alester Dayne's unexplained absence from court, and judged him ready for knighthood, though the Prince ultimately chose Ser Alester to perform the ceremony.

Held the White Sword Tower together through a poisoning attempt on the King, a vanished Crown Prince, and a sworn brother's unsanctioned flight from the capital — all within a single turbulent season.

oath: I shall keep the king I serve from harm, obey his commands, keep his secrets, counsel him when he asks it and no other time, and give my life for his if need be — forsaking lands, titles, and a wife of my own, all my days.

deeds.description: Served as Prince Jacaelon Targaryen's closest protector and confidant.
Uncovered Princess Rhaella's secret correspondence with her exiled kin during the poisoning crisis.
Defied the Hand of the King himself to guard a secret entrusted to him.
Escorted Crown Prince Visenor and Princess Rhaella out of King's Landing under cover of darkness, smuggling them toward Dorne.

Ambushed in the woods south of the capital by the disgraced former knight Curtass Whent and five hired sellswords; fought through brutal, sustained violence—losing an eye's worth of vision and half his ribs—before killing Whent in a final, desperate act to win free.
Knighted Prince Jacaelon Targaryen in a private ceremony, witnessed by no other, after returning to court.
Sought out by Princess Saera upon his battered return to the capital, quietly resuming their old habit of walking the corridors together late at night.

oath: I shall keep the king I serve from harm, obey his commands, keep his secrets, counsel him when he asks it and no other time, and give my life for his if need be — forsaking lands, titles, and a wife of my own, all my days.

deeds.description: Sworn shield to Princess Maela Targaryen.
Broke his sacred vows for her in a single night neither has spoken of since.
Continues in her service, wracked with guilt, the secret kept by the Princess's word alone and a cup of moon tea.

oath: I shall keep the king I serve from harm...

deeds.description: Served as a sworn brother of the Kingsguard with a fearsome reputation as a swordsman.
Left the order under clouded circumstances, his honor abandoned.
Summoned back by King Baelenys as a hired hunter to track down Crown Prince Visenor.
Ambushed Ser Alester Dayne and the fugitive Prince in the Dornish woods with five sellswords.
Slain by Ser Alester Dayne during the ambush, his body left near a rural sept.

notes: Struck from the White Book's place of honor; recorded here only as a warning.
